channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: AI Agents Are Coming for Your Dating Life
item.description: The developers of Pixel Societies are using AI agents to simulate social interactions. It's an attempt optimize the process of choosing new colleagues, friends, and even romantic partners.

item.title: 【ABC453 D】Go Straight 復習メモ：BFSの状態管理とTLE対策
item.description: まずは結論から。 この問題は単純なBFSではなく、「直前の移動方向」も状態に含めて管理する必要があった。 また、Pythonで実装する場合、訪問管理に set() を使うとTLEになるため、3次元配列による定数倍の高速化が必須だった。 コンテスト中に考えたこと 幅優先探索（BFS）または深さ優先探索（DFS）で解けそう。 しかし、前回動いた方向に依存するマス（o や x）がある。...

item.title: How I Built a Multi-Agent Code Review Pipeline That Actually Works
item.description: The Problem with Single-Agent Code Review Every team knows code review is essential. But every team also knows it\'s a bottleneck. You push a PR, wait hours for review, get feedback, push fixes,...

item.title: All It Took Was npm install (Axios Attack)
item.description: All it took was npm install. During the Axios attack, that was enough to run malicious code on your machine. In late March 2026, one of the most widely used JavaScript libraries in the world,...

item.title: How to Highlight Duplicates in Excel: A Developer-Friendly Guide
item.description: If you've ever received a spreadsheet from a product manager and been asked to "just check for duplicates," you know the feeling — it sounds trivial, but the moment you open the file, you realize...

item.title: Microsoft Auth in Next.js Finally Makes Sense
item.description: Trusted by 4,500+ developers, @chemmangat/msal-next turns one of the most frustrating parts of the Next.js App Router into a 5-minute setup. Integrating Azure AD/Entra ID shouldn't be this hard....

item.title: JSON to TypeScript: Generate Types in 5 Seconds
item.description: JSON to TypeScript: Generate Types in 5 Seconds Published on: Dev.to Tags: #typescript #vscode #productivity #code-generation #extension Reading time: 5 min The Problem: Manual Type Definition is...

item.title: quick feedback post
item.description: hi everyone! hope you've been doing alright. this is just a quickie post where i'd like to know my audience. so i could serve you better. the interaction with my last few posts has been very less,...

item.title: How Designers Are Making Motion Graphics Without Touching After Effects
item.description: Something has shifted in the motion graphics world. A few years ago, the assumption was simple: if you make motion graphics professionally, you use After Effects. The tool was so dominant that the...

item.title: Getting Started with Terraform: From Zero to Production
item.description: Infrastructure has undergone a fundamental shift over the past decade. What was once configured manually through dashboards and shell access is now defined declaratively in code. This shift is not...

item.title: Sam Altman reportedly targeted in second attack
item.description: Sam Altman was seemingly targeted by a second attack on Sunday morning, with two suspects arrested following a shooting at his Russian Hill residence, The San Francisco Standard reports. The...

item.title: How to Remove EXIF Data and Protect Your Privacy
item.description: What is EXIF data? EXIF stands for Exchangeable Image File Format. It is a standard for embedding technical metadata directly inside digital image files- specifically JPEG, TIFF, and RAW formats....

item.title: Huawei beats Apple and Samsung with new wide foldable
item.description: Apple and Samsung have both been strongly linked with plans for foldable phones in a boxy, wide aspect ratio, but it looks like another company will get there first. Huawei has just revealed the...

item.title: The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business
item.description: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.

item.title: OpenAI Revokes macOS App Certificate After Malicious Axios Supply Chain Incident
item.description: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps, which downloaded the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised....

item.title: Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model
item.description: The report is particularly surprising since the Department of Defense recently declared Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

item.title: Room for the Moon is thrillingly weird experimental pop
item.description: For obvious reasons, I've had Moon on the mind all week. So I was trying to figure out what I should recommend this week that would thematically fit. Brian Eno's Apollo: Atmospheres and...

item.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
item.description: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

item.title: Rockstar Games says hack will have ‘no impact’
item.description: Rockstar confirmed on Saturday that some of its data was compromised in a breach of a third-party provider. The group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility, saying it had gained access to the...

item.title: You can save $20 on the Super Mario Galaxy game bundle when you buy a Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: In celebration of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie coming to theaters, Nintendo is making it a little cheaper to get both Super Mario Galaxy games when you buy a $449.99 Nintendo Switch 2 console. Now...

item.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: At the HumanX conference, everyone was talking about Claude
item.description: Anthropic was the star of the show at San Francisco's AI-centric conference.

item.title: You can grab a refurbished 2021 Kindle Paperwhite starting at just $49.99
item.description: We spend a lot of time at The Verge waxing poetic about the latest gadgets, but sometimes it’s the last-gen devices from several years ago that offer the better value. The 2021 Kindle Paperwhite...

item.title: The Hisense UR9 is a great first shot against OLED’s bow
item.description: RGB LED TVs have been the talk of the TV world this year, with models coming from all the manufacturers, and the first one of 2026 is here - the Hisense UR9. It's the first look at the viability...

item.title: How AT&T created the most iconic phone ever
item.description: For years, even decades, virtually everyone in the United States had the same phone. Nobody really thought about it, it didn't even matter what it was called - it was just The Phone. Well, The...

item.title: The AI code wars are heating up
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the AI coding and vibe-coding booms, follow David Pierce. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: Allow me to explain why I love this camera that can’t shoot color
item.description: I love black-and-white photography. I also adore compact cameras you can always have by your side. So I'm a total mark for the Ricoh GR IV Monochrome, a fixed-lens camera that can't zoom and can't...

item.title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to...

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
item.description: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier...

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.
